data.title: Elon Musk Slams OpenAI's 'Non-Profit' Status for Being The 'Exact Opposite' of What He Funded
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) & SpaceX (SPCX) CEO Elon Musk said that OpenAI changed into an $800 billion closed-source business. He called OpenAI's shift the "exact opposite" of what he funded.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Two ETFs for long or short leverage on SPCX

Speaking with The Economist, Musk shared his issue with OpenAI, saying, "If you started a non-profit that was meant to be an open source AI company owned by the world and it somehow got turned into an $800 billion for-profit company with closed source, I think you'd be like, 'well, wait a second, that's the exact opposite of what I donated the money for.' That's my issue. I think it's a legitimate one."

His comments show why he continues his legal fight against OpenAI.

Musk Wanted OpenAI to Be a Free Alternative

Musk helped launch OpenAI in 2015 to create a free alternative to Google's (GOOGL) technology. He gave less than $45 million to get OpenAI moving before he stepped away in 2018.

OpenAI changed its plan completely late last year when OpenAI changed into a for-profit business. Tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) bought a 27% stake in OpenAI. Today, TipRanks data estimates put OpenAI's overall value at $750 billion, $50 million less than what Musk's estimate is.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: XRP's Price Is Struggling to Connect to Ripple Despite 'Ripple Mint' Launch and New Partnerships
data.description:
XRP's price (XRP-USD) struggles to connect to Ripple despite new partnership deals designed to bring big institutions into the fold. The company recently launched Ripple Mint, a single dashboard that makes creating and redeeming Ripple USD (RLUSD) simpler for large firms. It also backed compliance firm Notabene to add identity checks directly to payments.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

While these new partnership deals widen Ripple's enterprise footprint, XRP's market price continues to move independently from corporate news.

Updates Make RLUSD More Attractive

Ripple Mint replaces older system processes with a single console and direct automated tools. The system sends real-time alerts so big trading firms can match buy and sell orders right away. These firms, known as market makers, act as the buyers and sellers that keep markets fluid. Giving them live updates means they can trade RLUSD faster and with far fewer delays.

At the same time, the Notabene deal brings bank-grade identity checks to fund transfers. Together, these updates make RLUSD more attractive to regulated financial firms across global markets.

Could RLUSD Help XRP Grow Over Time?

In theory, expanding RLUSD can support XRP over time. The stablecoin now operates across five separate blockchains, including the XRP Ledger's EVM sidechain.

Every transaction on the XRP Ledger burns a fraction of XRP as a network fee. This slowly shrinks the total supply of XRP over time. Ripple says using RLUSD on the ledger drives more activity, which supports XRP in the long run.

XRP's Price Isn't Correlating to Ripple's Moves

Despite these new partnership deals, XRP is trading near $1.12. It seems to show little reaction to the Ripple Mint platform launch. For quite a while, investors have noticed that Ripple's corporate news no longer moves the price of XRP like it used to.

One could blame RLUSD itself for widening this divide. Since big firms can settle payments using dollar-backed stablecoins instead of buying volatile cryptos, corporate progress no longer guarantees price gains for XRP.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Disney and Kraft Heinz Team Up on Multiyear Theme Park Partnership
data.description:
Walt Disney (DIS) and Kraft Heinz (KHC) have launched a new multiyear agreement that puts ten packaged food brands directly into Disney's theme parks, cruise ships, and media platforms. The partnership brings household staples like Heinz ketchup, Kraft Macaroni & Cheese, and Philadelphia cream cheese to hundreds of locations across Disney World and Disneyland.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The alliance aims to connect popular grocery items with family vacations while helping both firms tackle weak stock performance.

Kraft Heinz Is Taking Over as Disney's Top Food Supplier

Kraft Heinz is taking over as the top food supplier across Disney's North American parks and cruises. Guests will see new sauce dispensers and custom dishes at Disney World, Disneyland, and on Disney Cruise ships.

The deal also extends into Disney's media segment. Both companies plan to create joint ad campaigns and custom event offerings, including branded sauce stations at Disney's upcoming D23 fan event.

Disney Can Boost Park Dining Without Risking Its Own Cash

Putting familiar foods in Disney parks gives Kraft Heinz a chance to make its classic brands feel popular again. Many food companies are losing sales to cheaper store brands. This makes theme park spots a smart way to get families excited about their products.

This partnership aligns with Disney's strategy of bringing in corporate sponsors to boost park dining without risking its own cash. Letting Kraft Heinz supply famous food brands gives Disney better park menus while sharing the cost of ads and new products.

Wall Street Still Wants Better Financial Results

Neither company shared specific financial terms, leading investors to treat the deal as a long-term branding effort rather than an immediate boost to profits. Shares for both firms showed little movement following the announcement, reflecting broader market caution.

Disney stock faces pressure over domestic park attendance and media shifts, while Kraft Heinz works to reverse falling sales volume. Wall Street analysts note that while the partnership builds brand visibility, its true impact will depend on future per-guest spending and sales trends.

What Do Wall Street Analysts Think of DIS and KHC Stock?

According to the TipRanks Stock Comparison tool, Wall Street analysts hold a Strong Buy consensus on Disney (DIS). Their average price target of $130.11 points to a potential 40.16% upside. The stock also earns a high Smart Score of 9 out of 10.

Kraft Heinz stock (KHC), on the other hand, carries a Hold consensus with a $23.00 price target. This suggests a potential 9.31% downside risk and a Smart Score of 6. However, its 6.16% dividend yield is much higher than Disney's 1.56%.

Moreover, we can see that Disney currently trades near $92.83 with a market cap of $166.48 billion, while Kraft Heinz sits at $25.36 with a market cap of $30.78 billion.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Stock Futures Wobble as Oil Supply Fears Lift Crude Prices
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded mixed ahead of Friday's session after a sharp selloff on Wall Street, as investors remained cautious amid rising oil prices. The ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict and concerns over oil supply disruptions continued to weigh on market sentiment. Brent crude briefly topped $100 a barrel on Thursday for the first time in two months before retreating.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) were down 0.19%, while those on the S&P 500 Index (SPX) and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were marginally up 0.02% and 0.08%, respectively, at 1:47 a.m. EDT on July 24.

Oil prices were set for weekly gains after Houthi attacks on tankers in the Red Sea raised concerns about disruptions to another important oil shipping route. Kazakhstan's temporary production cuts also supported prices. At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) traded at $96.23 a barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) was at $92.11 a barrel.

During Thursday's regular trading session, major U.S. stock indices fell sharply as investors worried about rising AI spending following earnings reports from Tesla (TSLA) and Alphabet (GOOGL). The Nasdaq dropped 2.15%, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 1.21% and 0.97%, respectively. The major indices are now on track for weekly losses, with the Dow, the S&P 500, and the Nasdaq down 0.8%, 0.7%, and 1.5%, respectively.

On the earnings front, results from Verizon (VZ), American Express (AXP), NextEra Energy (NEE), and SLB (SLB) are scheduled to report results today.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: SpaceX Stock Drops as Weather Delays Starship Flight 13 Launch
data.description:
SpaceX (SPCX) stock fell 1.73% in after-hours trading on Thursday as the company delayed Starship Flight 13 due to poor weather. The launch is now scheduled for Friday, July 24. SpaceX said the current weather conditions would not allow it to collect the data, but it expects better visibility for Friday's launch attempt.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction SPCX bulls now have this Tradr ETF

For context, Starship Flight 13 is the 13th test launch of SpaceX's Starship rocket, the world's largest and most powerful launch system. It includes the Super Heavy booster and the Starship spacecraft, both designed to be fully reusable. Since 2023, the company has completed 12 test flights as it works toward future missions to Earth orbit, the Moon, and Mars.

Why Was the Launch Delayed?

This is the second attempt at Starship Flight 13. The first launch was aborted on July 16 just before liftoff after several Raptor engines failed. SpaceX later replaced two engines and cleared the rocket for another launch attempt.

The latest delay is due to poor weather. SpaceX said it needs clear skies to capture ground images of Starship's heat shield during launch. Current weather conditions would not allow the company to collect that data, so the launch was pushed back.

What It Means for Investors

For investors, Starship is a key part of SpaceX's long-term growth story. A successful Flight 13 would deploy 20 next-generation Starlink V3 satellites for the first time, an important step in expanding the Starlink network. Starship is also expected to support future NASA missions and commercial launches while lowering the cost of sending payloads into space.

A successful Flight 13 would boost confidence that SpaceX is making steady progress. A single test flight is unlikely to have a big near-term impact. But each successful launch moves SpaceX closer to its long-term goals.

What Is SPCX's Stock Price Target?

SPCX stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks based on 23 Buys, five Holds, and one Sell assigned after its IPO. The average price target for SpaceX is $243.81, implying an upside of 106.2% from the current price.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Flat After a Weak Trading Session
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were near the flatline on Thursday evening after a rough day for markets as Tesla (TSLA) and Alphabet's (GOOGL) earnings and a sharp jump in oil prices weighed on market sentiment. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were up 0.15% and 0.02%, respectively, at 6:11 p.m. EDT on July 23, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) futures were down 0.02%.

data.title: 3 Income ETFs With Yields Above 20% for Investors to Watch
data.description:
Income‑focused ETFs have been gaining attention as more investors look for steady cash payouts in a volatile market. Some option‑income funds currently offer yields above 20%, driven by covered-call strategies and active options writing. According to TipRanks' ETF Screener, the YieldMax AI & Tech Portfolio Option Income ETF (GTPY), NestYield Dynamic Income Shield ETF (EGGY), and REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF (FEPI) all offer dividend yields above 20%.

High conviction AMZN bears now have this Tradr ETF

1. YieldMax AI & Tech Portfolio Option Income ETF (GTPY)

    GTPY focuses on big AI and tech names such as Nvidia (NVDA), Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOGL), and AMD (AMD). Also, it uses an options strategy to turn market swings into weekly income. Since it writes calls on a basket of high‑volatility tech stocks, the fund can generate a very high yield. The trade‑off is that it may give up some upside if tech stocks run sharply higher.

    The ETF makes weekly payouts. Its latest payment was $0.281 per share on July 23 and has paid $15.62 per share over the past year. The ETF has fallen about 11% over the past year.

data.title: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) Gains After Launching New Server Line Powered by AMD EPYC 9006 CPUs
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (SMCI) rose over 2% on Thursday after the company introduced its new H15 server portfolio built on AMD's (AMD) latest 6th‑generation EPYC 9006 Series processors. The launch expands Supermicro's data center lineup at a time when demand for high-performance compute systems continues to surge. Also, the rollout strengthens Supermicro's position in the fast‑growing AI infrastructure market.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The new H15 systems feature up to 256 cores and 512 threads, offering a major jump in compute power for customers running agentic AI, high‑performance computing, storage, and large enterprise applications.

The H15 line covers several system types, including dual‑socket Hyper servers for AI inference and cloud work, CloudDC systems built on OCP standards, high‑density GrandTwin and FlexTwin designs for hyperscale sites, and new Petascale Storage platforms for AI data lakes and analytics. Supermicro also updated its AMD GPU systems, adding new 5U PCIe servers that can support up to ten AMD Instinct MI350P GPUs.

Wide Range of H15 Systems and New AMD GPU Servers

Supermicro said the platform delivers a 1.7x performance boost over the prior generation. It also offers expanded memory bandwidth and improved compute density. The company said these gains help customers run more AI agents at the same time and speed up their business apps.

Vik Malyala, the company's Chief Business Officer, said the new AMD‑powered systems bring "high performance, rapid scalability, and peak efficiency" to customers deploying AI at scale. Also, AMD's Dan McNamara, general manager of Compute and Enterprise AI, noted that pairing EPYC CPUs with Instinct GPUs and Pensando networking helps enterprises deploy AI faster while lowering energy use and total cost.

Beyond individual servers, Supermicro is working with AMD on the Helios Platform, a rack‑scale system featuring 72 GPUs for large AI training jobs. The liquid‑cooled design combines EPYC CPUs, Instinct GPUs, Pensando networking, and AMD's ROCm software stack to support frontier‑level AI workloads.

Is SMCI a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, SMCI stock has received a Hold consensus rating, with four Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. The average price target for Super Micro Computer is $37.91, suggesting an upside of 21.50% from the current level.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Microsoft to Report Q4 Earnings on July 29; Here's Why Truist Is Bullish on MSFT Stock
data.description:
All eyes are now on tech giant Microsoft's (MSFT) fiscal fourth-quarter earnings on July 29 after Alphabet's (GOOGL) Q2 2026 print spooked investors due to concerns about its elevated capital spending. Heading into Q4 FY26 earnings, Truist analyst Terry Tillman reiterated a Buy rating on MSFT stock with a price target of $575. While MSFT stock has declined 21% year-to-date amid worries about returns on the company's massive AI spending, Tillman contends that the "Negative Sentiment Feels Overdone." He expects Microsoft to report "solid" Q4 performance and issue a strong outlook.
